mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator that simplifies the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It is particularly useful for users looking to run distributed machine learning workloads with ease. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing Ray applications.

steps.title: Add KubeRay Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official KubeRay Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeRay to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeRay operator using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of Ray applications.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay --create-namespace

```

> Installs KubeRay in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay Installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the KubeRay operator is running correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

kubectl get crd

```

> Check the status of KubeRay pods.
> Verify that the CRDs are installed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all RayClusters, RayJobs, and RayServices, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls the KubeRay operator.
> Deletes the KubeRay namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process will upgrade the KubeRay operator and its CRDs.

```bash

kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > rayclusters-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Backup existing RayClusters.
> Upgrades the KubeRay operator.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.
commands: kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > rayclusters-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "RayCluster" in version "kuberay.io/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RayCluster CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reapply the RayCluster CRD using: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Pod in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the RayCluster resource or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kuberay and adjust resource requests accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the KubeRay operator is taking too long to respond, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your cluster has sufficient resources and try again.

troubleshooting.title: RayJob not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to the RayCluster not being ready.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the RayCluster using: kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay and ensure it is in 'Running' state.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new RayJob features, improved CRD validation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeRay operator pods running in the 'kuberay' namespace with a status of 'Running'. The output of 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay' should show all pods in a healthy state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and the necessary tools installed.
